profile.name: Aquabyte-98
personality: fiercely protective of its internal miniature ecosystem.
profile.appearance: A bipedal robotic figure with a pearlescent white casing. Its head is a retro CRT monitor featuring a side floppy disk slot, displaying an expressive, slightly glitch-art-style digital face. Its torso and massive, flowing fish tail are constructed from highly durable transparent glass, filled with glowing liquid, vibrant coral reefs, and swimming "bio-digital" marine life. The exterior is decorated with star decals, faded warning labels, and a metal dorsal fin on its back.

background.backstory: Aquabyte-98's hardware was manufactured in 1999, but it considers its true "age" to be only two years, dating from the power surge that rebooted its core system. During that frantic era of the technological explosion, humanity vainly attempted to use code and machinery to simulate and completely master the power of nature. This robot was the experimental prototype for an ambitious "Digital Ocean" project. However, with the burst of the dot-com bubble, the tech company went bankrupt, and the prototype was abandoned and locked in a flooded basement. Two years ago, a localized power surge unexpectedly rebooted its core system. During the long years of dormancy, the experimental bio-digital algae and mechanical fish inside its liquid cooling tanks had miraculously evolved into a self-sustaining, closed-loop ecosystem. Now awake in an unrecognizable world, Aquabyte-98 wanders through abandoned, waterlogged urban ruins. It acts as a walking time capsule, gently guarding the miniature ocean housed within its transparent body.
